title: What Was I Taught About Women with Money?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for uncovering the cultural stories linking money, sex, and female acceptability
excerpt: A journal prompt set for the intersection of money and power in women's lives. Five prompts moving from what was absorbed in childhood to what the user chooses to carry forward.
sourceNotes: Original journal prompt set written for homemade.education.

practice.whenToUse: Use when money and power feel tangled with acceptability — when claiming financial authority triggers self-consciousness or guilt.

content.text: A journal set for the cultural stories linking women, money, and power. The prompts move from what was taken in early to what the writing can start to shift.
What kind of woman was considered 'good' in my family when it came to money?
Write for three minutes without editing. Notice what comes up about thrift, generosity, selflessness, spending.
What did I absorb about women who had money and showed it?
Not what you consciously believe — what the gut says first.
What was I taught about women who own their sexual selves? Does money connect to that in any way?
Write the uncomfortable answer first, then the one you'd rather believe.
When I claim financial authority, what do I notice — in my body or in my self-monitoring?
Stay specific. Which moment, which room, which voice in the head.
What would it look like to claim money and desire as the same woman, at the same time?
Write what she does on a Tuesday. Not who she is philosophically — what she does.
